description: A 'sitawi' is a record of items held within a stated scope at a stated time. It may include their places, amounts, or conditions. The inventory represents 'muphera' (stock) without becoming it; the shelf keeps changing even when the record does not.
articulatory_notes: Bring the tongue close to the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's' and release into high-front 'i'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't', then open into stressed central 'a'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w' before final high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: Fine hissing 'si' meets the broad dental beat of stressed 'ta'. Final 'wi' rounds the lips and draws the sound back towards a bright close. The wide centre folds neatly inward, like many held things gathered into one record.
usage_notes: Inventories are countable. Scope and time belong close to any claim made from one, while method and recorder may matter when the record is disputed. 'sirami' is the wider record category, and 'tawi' is the act of counting.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A shared inventory can make material capacity available for repair and reuse while remaining answerable to what is physically present.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A time-bounded inventory invites correction instead of letting an old record become unquestionable proof of what exists or who controls it.

semantic_domains.cognition: a structured representation of material on hand
semantic_domains.temporal: a record bounded to the time it describes
